etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "frisk" + "-y" from Middle French "frisque" (lively, brisk)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Lively brisk quality; having playful energy and frisky playfulness with energetic presence
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Lively brisk → playful energy → contemporary frisky playfulness and frisky energy with playful presence
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Lively brisk → playful energy → frisky playfulness → playful understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Having frisky playfulness and frisky energy; experiencing playful energy and frisky friskiness with frisky presence
contemporary_usage.register: Positive; indicates playfulness and energetic liveliness with spirited energy

- **Frequency Trend**: Medium; common for expressing playful states and energetic experiences
